pi_items.source: Verified through hazmat_incident_report.txt (line 16) and CAMEO Chemicals NOAA database (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/2862)

Verified through hazmat_incident_report.txt (line 17) and multiple authoritative sources: CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/unna/1017), HazMat Tool (https://www.hazmattool.com/info.php?language=en&a=Chlorine&b=UN1017&c=2.3)

Verified through multiple authoritative sources: CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/unna/1017), Environmental Chemistry ERG database (https://environmentalchemistry.com/yogi/hazmat/erg/gn/124.html), HazMat Tool (https://www.hazmattool.com/emergencyguide.php?language=en&i=124). ERG 124 is titled 'Gases - Toxic and/or Corrosive - Oxidizing' and applies to Chlorine UN 1017.
Verified through task.json description (line 5) and setup_task.sh (line 25), which consistently references this output path for saving emergency response findings

additional_verified_facts.incident_scenario: Fictional tanker truck incident (HZ-2025-04817) with MC-331 pressurized cargo tank containing 18,000 kg of Chlorine, with damage to rear valve assembly

privileged_info_summary: The task involves looking up Emergency Response Guide information for Chlorine (UN 1017) using the real NOAA CAMEO Chemicals website. All metadata claims are verified as correct: Chlorine is UN 1017, which corresponds to ERG Guide 124 ('Gases - Toxic and/or Corrosive - Oxidizing'). For large spills, the initial isolation distance is 600 feet with protective action distances of 3.5 km during day and 8 km during night. The incident data is synthetic, describing a fictional tanker truck incident with specific hazmat details. The task directs agents to use the real CAMEO Chemicals website (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/) to research emergency response procedures and save findings to ~/Documents/chlorine_emergency_response.txt.
